name: triage-v2
description: Triages incoming issues, filters duplicates or unclear reports, assesses fixability, and produces candidate work items.

coder.prompt_template:
List open bug issues for {{project.owner}}/{{project.repo}} (job payload: {{job_payload}}).

First try label-aware:
  gh issue list --repo {{project.owner}}/{{project.repo}} --state open --label bug --limit 50 --json number,title,url,labels,assignees,createdAt,updatedAt,author,comments
If the repo does not use 'bug' label, fall back to a search query:
  gh issue list --repo {{project.owner}}/{{project.repo}} --state open --search 'created:>=<since_days_ago> bug OR crash OR panic OR regression OR fails' --limit 50 --json ...

Use the job_payload.since_days (default 2), .max_issues (default 50), and .labels (default ['bug']) if set.

Save the raw JSON list to /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-issue-list.json. Print a short numbered summary (issue # + title + URL).

STANCE: issues_found | empty | gh_unavailable | failed

For each issue URL in /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-issue-list.json, fetch its body + comments:
  gh issue view <N> --repo {{project.owner}}/{{project.repo}} --json number,title,url,state,body,labels,assignees,author,comments,createdAt,updatedAt

Aggregate to /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-issue-details.json (one entry per issue).

If at least one fetched successfully but some failed, return `partial`. If all failed, return `fetch_failed`.

STANCE: details_ready | partial | fetch_failed

Read /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-issue-details.json. For each issue, classify `kept` vs `dropped`.

Deterministic drops (don't waste budget on agent classification):
- labels include any of: enhancement, feature, feature-request, question, discussion, docs, documentation, help-wanted (when standalone), wontfix, invalid
- title or body explicitly says 'feature request', 'support question', 'how do I', 'discussion'

Kept criteria (agent body-read for ambiguous):
- describes incorrect behavior, crash, regression, data loss, test failure, or API inconsistency
- has expected vs actual behavior, repro snippet, stack trace, failing command, or enough code pointers
- not a pure feature request, open-ended design discussion, support question, dependency upgrade, or vague complaint

Emit /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-filtered.json with per-issue: `{ url, kept: true|false, reason, confidence: 0..1, evidence: "short body excerpt" }`.

If you cannot classify >50% of issues, return `needs_human`.

STANCE: kept_some | none_kept | needs_human

For each kept issue from /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-filtered.json, check claim signals using the data already in /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-issue-details.json:

Mark per issue:
- `open_unclaimed`: state open, no assignees, no linked open PR, no maintainer 'I'll take this' / 'working on it' / 'assigned to me', no recent active claim from a contributor
- `assigned`: has assignees
- `claimed`: maintainer/contributor comment indicates active work
- `closed`: state is closed (sanity check)
- `needs_human`: signals conflict (e.g. assigned but assignee says 'help wanted')

Emit /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-claim-check.json.

STANCE: unclaimed_some | all_claimed | needs_human

For each `open_unclaimed` issue from /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-claim-check.json, run lmdedup:
  lmdedup --repo {{project.owner}}/{{project.repo}} --issue-url <ISSUE_URL> --json

Notes:
- GITHUB_TOKEN must be set; lmdedup also needs 30+ min timeout for large corpora — use `timeout 1800 lmdedup ...` if your shell respects it.
- Save each per-issue lmdedup JSON to /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-lmdedup-<issue_number>.json.

Aggregate to /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-dedupe.json with per issue: `{ url, dedupe_outcome: distinct|duplicate|active_pr_exists|ambiguous, evidence }`.

STANCE: dedupe_done | dedupe_failed

For each `distinct` issue from /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-dedupe.json, score repro clarity from the body in /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-issue-details.json:

- 5: exact repro command/test/case
- 4: clear code path + expected/actual
- 3: enough detail for a repo expert to reproduce
- 2: plausible but missing setup
- 1: vague
- 0: not reproducible from report

Classification:
- score >= 3 → `clear`
- score == 2 → `needs_human`
- score < 2 → `unclear` (will be skipped downstream)

Emit /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-repro-clarity.json.

STANCE: scored | needs_human

`cd {{project.local_path}}` first. If that directory does not contain a checkout of {{project.owner}}/{{project.repo}}, `git clone https://github.com/{{project.owner}}/{{project.repo}}.git {{project.local_path}}` first.

For each `clear`-scored issue from /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-repro-clarity.json:
- read the body from /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-issue-details.json
- inspect likely files via `rg`/`grep`/`cat` (do NOT edit)
- identify suspected touchpoints (file + approximate line)
- identify the test framework and likely focused test command
- do NOT run a full build

Emit /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-fixability.json per issue: `{ url, likely_files: [..], test_command: "npm test" | "go test ./..." | ..., risk: "low|medium|high", estimated_size: "small|medium|large", notes }`.

Classification:
- file pointers identified + test command found → `local_and_testable`
- file pointers identified but tests unclear → `local_but_tests_unclear`
- broad/risky scope → `broad_or_risky`
- ambiguous → `needs_human`

STANCE: assessed | needs_human

`cd {{project.local_path}}` first.

Verify:
1. Git checkout exists (`git rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree` returns true).
2. Worktree is clean enough for branching (`git status --short` empty, unless job_payload.allow_existing_clone_dirty=true).
3. Toolchain present for the test_command identified in fix_locality_and_test_availability — try `node --version`, `go version`, `python --version`, `cargo --version`, etc. as appropriate.

Do NOT run a full build.

STANCE: ready | missing_clone | dirty_worktree | toolchain_missing | needs_human

Reduce all prior per-issue artifacts into a single triage_results.json.

For each issue from /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-issue-details.json:
- Join filtered.json + claim-check.json + dedupe.json + repro-clarity.json + fixability.json.
- Status mapping:
  - `candidate`: kept + open_unclaimed + distinct + repro >= 3 + (local_and_testable or local_but_tests_unclear)
  - `assigned`: assignees present OR maintainer claim
  - `dup`: duplicate OR active_pr_exists
  - `skip`: non-bug OR repro < 2 OR broad_or_risky OR closed
  - `needs_human`: ambiguous dup OR repro == 2 OR conflicting signals

Write the final aggregate to /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-triage_results.json (NOT inside {{project.local_path}} — triage MUST NOT dirty the target repo checkout).

Return STANCE: candidates_found (>=1 candidate), empty (no candidates), or needs_operator_review (mixed signals).

agent.initial_prompt_template: Triage produced candidates. Review /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-triage_results.json (run artifact area; the target repo at {{project.local_path}} is left clean). Choose: approve_selected (write /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-approved.json listing issue URLs to fix), approve_all_candidates (all status=candidate), reject_all, or needs_more_triage. Signal done when decided.

coder.prompt_template:
The operator has reviewed the triage candidates. Based on the conversation context, determine their decision.

If the operator approved selected issues (wrote /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-approved.json): STANCE: approve_selected
If the operator approved all candidates: STANCE: approve_all_candidates
If the operator rejected all: STANCE: reject_all
If the operator wants more triage: STANCE: needs_more_triage

Operator outcome: {{outcomes.operator_review_candidates_decide}}.

Determine the approved issue URL set:
- If outcome was `approve_all_candidates`: take every issue with status `candidate` from /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-triage_results.json.
- If outcome was `approve_selected`: read /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-approved.json (operator-written list of URLs).

For each approved URL, enqueue a bugfix-pr-fixer job. The engine parses fenced ```spawn_jobs``` blocks from your reply — emit ONE such block containing a JSON array, one entry per approved URL:

```spawn_jobs
[
  {"project_id": {{project.id}}, "payload": {"issue_url": "<URL1>"}},
  {"project_id": {{project.id}}, "payload": {"issue_url": "<URL2>"}}
]
```

If no URLs are approved, return STANCE: nothing_to_enqueue and emit no spawn_jobs block.
On success, STANCE: enqueued.
On parse/IO failure, STANCE: failed.
